core_concept: Same and Different: Sorting and Classifying
facts: Two objects are the same color when they both have that color, like a red apple and a red ball — they belong in a group of red things.
Sorting means putting objects into groups by noticing one shared attribute, such as putting all square blocks together and all round blocks together.
An object can be different from another object in size even if they are the same shape; a big circle and a small circle are different in size but same in shape.
misconceptions: A learner might think that objects can only be considered 'same' if they match in every possible way (color, shape, and size), not understanding that 'same' can refer to just one shared attribute like color.
